findings: Identity is correct: the plate depicts a breeding male Mallard with one head, one broad yellow bill, two legs with orange webbed feet, one short tail with curled black central feathers, and two wings (the far wing is occluded in the lateral view).

Plumage and structure match the supplied Mallard references and live identification pages: iridescent green head, narrow white neck ring, chestnut breast, gray body, black rear, and blue speculum with white borders.

All visible text is accurate. Cornell lists Mallard as Anas platyrhynchos in Anatidae and gives the plate’s exact 50–65 cm length, 82–95 cm wingspan, and 1.0–1.3 kg weight ranges. Audubon independently corroborates the species, major field marks, habitat, and broadly overlapping size ranges.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears. No material species, anatomy, or text error found.
